title: Extended Kalman Filter (EKF)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Extended Kalman Filter (EKF): EKF: extend Kalman filter to nonlinear systems by linearizing dynamics and measurement functions via Jacobians. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (S.ekf.jacobian_linearization…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~100; strong_nonlinearity, bimodal_posterior set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: x_{k+1} = f(x_k, u_k) + w_k; y_k = h(x_k) + v_k; F_k = df/dx|_{x_hat_k}, H_k = dh/dx|_{x_hat_k}
E.world_state_x: nonlinear state estimate x_hat_k and covariance P_k for nonlinear system
E.observation_y: nonlinear measurements {y_k} from sensors (GPS, IMU, bearing angles, etc.)

W.regime: Existence of the recovered nonlinear_state_estimate is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 100); strong_nonlinearity dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: EKF suboptimal for strong nonlinearity; RMSE increases with nonlinearity degree. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~100. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |strong_nonlinearity|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(n ** 3 + n ** 2 * p) per step for Jacobian + update per iteration
